incidentDescription: Genevieve was eating her dinner when she momentarily choked on a piece of chicken. She coughed and threw up and the piece of chicken out removed. She drank water and was shaken but she recovered well.

residentsInvolved.name: Genevieve Begay
residentsInvolved.roleInIncident: The victim of the choking incident

actionsDescription:
1. Ensured Genevieve is okay
2. Monitor after-effect behavior
3. 1-1 discussion to uncover any other underlying cause
4. Food intake safety instruction review
residentCurrentCondition:
Genevieve reported not resting well through out the night
Genevieve continued to not feel well the following day.
residentStatement: "I just choked on the piece of chicken, I just didn't feel well afterwards"
followUpActionsTimeline:
1. Increased monitoring
2. Softer food provided for easier intake and overall diet monitoring
3. Rest encouragement/education
bhpSignatureName: Dr. Chris Azode, DNP, PMHNP-BC, MBA

incidentDescription:
Genevieve was not feeling well. She reported feeling weak, with a headache and very little to no appetite. 
Genevieve also reported getting heart palpitations.
Visually, Genevieve looked weak and unwell. 
Genevieve reported her thoughts that her illness was related to the chocking incident that she experienced the day prior. 
Staff member decided to take Genevieve to the hospital to be checked into ER or further check up.
hospitalName: Dignity Health Arizona General Hospital: Emergency Room
actionsDescription: Genevieve was immediately transported to the hospital.
residentCurrentCondition:
Genevieve was found to have a low count of red blood cells and severe anemia
Genevieve was given 2 pints of blood transfusion and monitored
Genevieve was later discharged with instructions to follow up in 1-3 days
followUpActionsTimeline:
1. 02/22/2026 11:00 PM - Genevieve picked up from hospital after discharge
2. 02/22/2026 11:15 PM - Genevieve is provided soft food and fluids before she goes to bed
3. 02/23/2026 9:00 AM - Genevieve wakes up rested and feeling better. 
4. 02/23/2026 10:00 AM - Genevieve's medication (ferrous sulphate) is not ready for pickup
5. 02/23/2026 11:00 AM - Staff member calls provider for Genevieve's follow up appointment
bhpSignatureName: Dr. Chris Azode, DNP, PMHNP-BC, MBA

incidentDescription:
On the morning of March 23, 2026, resident Genevieve Begay relapsed after taking her scheduled morning medications. The resident had secretly brought in and consumed a shot glass of liquor (alcohol).

Following the consumption of alcohol, the resident exhibited signs of intoxication and attempted to leave the facility. Staff member intervened and engaged in verbal de-escalation, successfully convincing the resident to remain at the facility.

However, after a few minutes, the resident changed her mind and stated her intention to leave the facility Against Medical Advice (AMA). Despite staff efforts to encourage her to stay, the resident was insistent on leaving.

The resident stated she was heading to the nearby Circle K gas convenience store. The resident left the facility premises while visibly intoxicated.

This incident represents a relapse in the resident's substance use recovery and an AMA departure from the facility.

STAFF ASSESSMENT:
Lucid Behavioral Health staff suspects that this incident may have been triggered by a recent family loss. Genevieve lost her cousin last week and has appeared to be emotionally shaken by this loss. The grief and emotional distress from this family member's death may have contributed to her relapse and subsequent decision to leave the facility.

DISCHARGE & REFERRAL:
Genevieve has been referred to Virtue Recovery Center for their Detox program. She will be discharged today (March 23, 2026) and will be transported to Virtue Recovery Center located at 13952 W Meeker Blvd, Sun City West, AZ 85375.

residentsInvolved.name: Genevieve Begay
residentsInvolved.roleInIncident: Primary subject - relapsed and left AMA

staffInvolved.name: Facility Staff
staffInvolved.title: BHRF Staff
staffInvolved.roleInIncident: Attempted verbal de-escalation and convinced resident to stay temporarily

actionsDescription: Staff member spoke with the resident and initially convinced her to stay at the facility. The resident agreed to remain but changed her mind after a few minutes and proceeded to leave the facility against medical advice.
residentConditionBeforeIncident: Genevieve was grieving the loss of her cousin, who passed away on the Friday prior to this incident. Since hearing the news, she had not been eating well, and staff believe her relapse may have started shortly after. On the evening of March 22, 2026, Genevieve appeared weak and sleepy and did not want to eat her dinner. A staff member spoke with her and explained the importance of eating a full meal, especially when feeling weak. Genevieve said she did not want the meal that was prepared but was willing to eat Asian food. A staff member then ordered food from Panda Express, and Genevieve was able to eat her dinner before going to sleep shortly after.
residentCurrentCondition:
Resident has been located and appeared in an intoxicated condition. She has slurred speech and looks tired. 
Resident has agreed to transfer to Virtue Recovery Center for Detox program. Discharge scheduled for today (March 23, 2026). Transportation arranged to Virtue Recovery Center at 13952 W Meeker Blvd, Sun City West, AZ 85375.
residentStatement: Resident initially agreed to stay after staff conversation but then changed her mind. She stated she wanted to leave and was going to the Circle K and would be returning back.
followUpActionsTimeline: Notify BHP and treatment team of relapse and AMA discharge. Resident has been referred to Virtue Recovery Center Detox program. Coordinate transportation to Virtue Recovery Center at 13952 W Meeker Blvd, Sun City West, AZ 85375. Send discharge summary and relevant medical records to Virtue Recovery Center. Document incident for regulatory compliance.
bhpSignatureName: Dr. Chris Azode, DNP, PMHNP-BC, MBA
